reviews.review_text:
I'll keep it very short. It's been years since release. Many features that didn't work when the half finished game was "released" have been fixed. That's good. 

Sadly the good news ends here. 
There is still plenty of bugs and the base game has been stripped to it's absolute minimum. It feels like playing vanilla City Skylines I all over again. 

It is very sad that a game has to rely on the modding community just to make it playable. A big thank you to all the modders that invest their free time to do this. This is well is slowly being ruined by the companies greed. Moving away from the Steam Workshop to an internal modding platform centralises control over mods and adds the possibility that the company will be able to exert more legal leverage over modders free work.

One of the best features from CS1, the industries DLC, has still not been implemented. Building manufacturer chains and specialized industry areas gave the game a modern ANNO feel and a welcome change from zoning the same random buildings over and over again. I don't want metal facilities next to my farm land. I want silos and manufacturing plants that make farming goods.

As it stands CS2 is just a worse CS1 right now.
The price has already dropped since I bought it and the game is on sale multiple times a year. I'm sure the price will drop even further if you wait longer. If you own CS1 - play that and if you don't have the industries DLC consider getting that instead. 
If you own neither and want to get into city builders I am sorry to tell you that your best bet is to either get CS1 or wait. There are many new city builders from other companies in the making that look very promising. 

I want to make clear that all my critique is about monetization strategies and rushed development. Do not direct your anger at the developers. 

A final statement to the managers and CEO's that make these decisions: 
Let's be very clear. CS1 costs about 400€ all DLC's included and even then the game is barely playable without any mods. CS2 is approaching the same price at a rapid pace with all the same issues. 
You are constantly pushing developers to make more paid content while relying on modders to fix issues for free. The pricing model is exploitative and anti-consumer.

History of Cities: Skylines and I:

>play the first game in 2015
>hate colossal order
>pc has pentium in it, 5 fps but somehow still enjoy it
>hate colossal order
>watch city planner plays for hours
>after years, Cities: Skylines 2 announces
>everyone is hyped, me aswell
>game releases
>it sucks
>hate colossal order
>hating the second game and keep playing the first game
>game gets updates and going better
>hate colossal order
>buy second game
>play it for hours, love it
>hate colossal order
>iceflake studios takes over, doing much more work in months than colossal order
>game gets better
>hate colossal order

the mixed reviews are from the initial release, the game is a lot more refined and is very enjoyable to play. It still has issues, but the new studio is putting in more effort to optimize the game and make it a really good game. I highly recommend this one over the first game if you can run it, as the QOL features are a must have and make the game 10x more enjoyable. 
I wouldn't buy the game full price, but on sale it's very much so worth it.

A lot of people are talking about how bad this game is and how terribly optimized it is, but as someone with almost 45 hours at the time of this review, i feel like this is partially unwarranted. I'm going to give you an in depth explanation of the game, the ups and the downs, and everything else you need to know to make an informed decision about this game. 

First lets address the negative reviews. I personally have had no experience with any crashes in all my time playing CS2, so I cannot properly respond to them. However I will put my own theories out there. Theory one is that your playing on high graphic settings, which puts immense strain on your GPU, which I will return to later. Theory two is that you either have super massive 1m+ population cities, or you have mods that make citizen AI more advanced. The next complaint is how taxing CS2 is on your GPU. Any game with high enough graphics settings will make your GPU heat up.

The game itself is definitely in need of lots more work, that I don't deny. However though, to say it is terrible due to citizen AI being broken, and a lot of these other claims isn't fair to the game, because for a casual player these problems have never came up for myself. I do play with mods, but even on its own, vanilla CS2 is plenty fun, and I have a whole city going and its up to half a million citizens.

CS2 is properly paced, contrary to what some people say about it. It doesn't take too long to get from a small town to having high density residential. Each resident has their own life, leading to even better progression because you not only need to handle expanding the city, but also rush hour, and just plain residents going to the store, or the park. The game certainly has better progression then CS1, and allows you to choose what service you want to upgrade. This creates really interesting cities, and no two games are every quite the same.

The main problems the game has is that it over promised on release, and still hasn't quite lived up to expectations.  On top of that the publisher, Paradox Interactive, are incredibly greedy, and more often then not, updates adding new features come out as DLCs. The game itself is hilariously overpriced, and as Dr Seuss put it, I wouldn't touch it with a ten foot pool. However, when on sale I would definitely recommend it. Personally I think a price tag of 29.99$ is much more suitable for the game, rather then the fifty were currently paying.

In the end I definitely say that Cities: Skylines 2 is worth getting, even in the state it is in, just wait until it gets on sale. However the purpose of this is not to tell you to buy it or not to buy it, like other reviews, but rather to help you to make a informed decision.

Booted up the game for the first time in literal years, disabled all mods, started a new map. Froze and crashed to desktop after under 5 minutes, before I even laid the first road. Restart the game, get an error that an old save (not loaded) was corrupt, so that got moved automatically. Okay. Start a new map again, back to not responding in under 5 minutes. I guess this game still isn't ready, I'll come back in another 3 years.

I really want this to be a good game, my little brother loves playing it, but I only have so much space on my smaller pc (waiting to get the gabe cube when it comes out). If paradox fixes the more game breaking bugs, then you can bet ill spend hours playing this.
Bad reviews are outdated, they have fixed the performance issues and I have had 0 crashes in 25+ hours. A lot of new features compared to CS1 and the new dev team is off to a great start.

Great game overall, it grow on you and you want to spend hours city building, but unfortunately once my game reaches 60k population simulation just freezes so u are unable to play properly. Paid money for a game I can't actually play :C Unless u got a NASA pc don't touch this game. I wanna go back to CS1 now but CS2 spoiled me unfortunately :C

I love like the gameplay so far, and surprisingly with a not so good computer I can run the game very well. And my only problem is how expensive the DLCs are, like bridges and ports, should already be in the base game, the fact I have to pay 20 dollars for more content that should be in is crazy.

The reasons why I like this type of game are there but the tech problems are the reasons why I could never recommend it. 
I bought it on launch ran into problems then returned it. Bought it again after giving them enough time to fix the problems, and its playable now but doesn't come near the enjoyment of the first one. Some systems just flat out don't work properly at all. My PC that runs cyberpunk at med/high just fine, starts to struggle when a city reaches 200k. It blows my mind to see how bad this has become. Speed controls just don't work properly at all, and the traffic Ai is worse then CS1. Please do better.

The game is good but it still has lots of problems like the huge performance issues(I get max 20fps while playing), traffic is always bad no matter what you do, also the paid DLCS that should be included in the price(the game is expensive 50 euro for a unity game? WTF), other than that the game is good plus the new devs are constantly updating it and im sure theyl fix it.

It has a lot of good features, but the constant game-breaking bugs make it impossible to play. My first save file randomly corrupted four hours in and I couldn't rescue it. My second save was going well until a giant pillar of water appeared and flooded the entire map. I searched the internet and found out that this bug has existed for years and there's no way to fix it. I hope the new studio is able to turn things around but as it stands this game is still not in a reasonably playable state.

Been playing city building games since Sim City way back in the day. This is a good upgrade to the first skylines the only complaint i have is pedestrian traffic it would be nice to be able to install elevators or stairs instead of the long ramps. apart from that it's come a long way from the first skylines game.

Underwhelming, kinda just feels like the first one made with better graphics. But just as the first game, it is very basic unless you pay for the DLC's that have all the actually enjoyable content. I don't know, just feels like another game where the developers want you to pay for the updates, just like the first game, The Sims, ATS, and any other game you can think of that just has an unnecessary amount of DLCs and Addons.

Do yourself a favor, the first game costs $20 less, just buy that one. It is just as good as this one, the graphics are just a little worse.

Currently unplayable for me. 

Paradox launcher is broken, won't reinstall, and the support page only shows me an unfiltered forum page of hundreds of people with mildly different issues and no clean explanation for how to do something that seems like it should be quite simple.

I loved the first game, and was really excited to play this game when it first came out. Pre-purchased it and was so happy to see some of my favorite youtubers play the demo. 

Unfortunately, I can't say the same now. 

The main problem I've been having is the Creator Packs from the Paradox Mods. I have been downloading modpacks for a more texturous city, however anytime I make a save, something get's corrupted and doesn't allow me to use the mods on that save anymore. I've searched everywhere for an answer, however I can't figure out if it's my pc or the game. 

As another note, I'm not happy with how they are selling DLCs as a more "give us your money" rather than "this offers a new experience." I just wish the devs would make it as part of the base game.

Nothing ever changes with this steaming pile. You give it many, many, many, many chances to come around and just when you think maybe we're on track - crash, failure to load, busted saves, crash, excessive load times, and on and on. Don't spend good money on this and more importantly, don't waste your time building and designing and detailing and giving transportation networks logical thought to watch it all go up in smoke. I've given this game more chances than it deserves. Please don't respond by saying just "play vanilla and you can avoid the majority of the problems" This game without mods and custom assets is awful. I'm finished here.
great game but its very expensive and some things in the game are unclear if you are going to play this game plan your road layout very very far ahead
I wouldnt have recommended it before, but with the new devs I do. the game is close to what peak cities 1 was like - just needs more time for mods and assets to catch up

Fun game in concept. in practice it is the most demanding game ive ever seen.... cant even get a city above 10k without reaching 20fps on medium settings and the game crashing corrupting all my progress.... just go play skyline 1 with mods or better yet something that Paradox Interactive has not touched

As of May 2026 I'm disappointed with city's 2, for a sequel to one of the biggest city building games its bland. They made a lot of improvements but once again are heavily relying on DLC and MODs to keep things fresh. The shear lack of options for buildings, parks, monuments, props, decals and services make every city lose its identity very fast with out mods.

Thank you Modder's for once again propping this game up with options.
the game always crashes after I try to play I also had to wait like 10 minutes for the game to eve load the menu .For Reference here are my specs AMD Ryzen 5600x ,32 gb of ram and an RTX 3060
A great experience, with Iceflake studios taking the lead i have seen major improvements to the game. However, focusing less on expansions and creator packs would be nice. 8/10

I finally bit the bullet and bought the game! Oh it sure is beautiful. I absolutely believe it surpasses the first game at this point. Really great modding community, too.

I've only encountered a few bugs and none of them were game breaking. Though I'll note that I play it on my husband's very impressive gaming PC because I use a Mac, hehe. So maybe take that into consideration.

This game is a slog! It's so hard to get through. It has zero charm. It is a disappointing, slow, unpolished, unfinished game that should have only launched through Steam Greenlight first before ever making the claim that it was finished.

Also, SimCity (2012) for all its flaws was actually not that bad a game, especially with Cities of Tomorrow. Now that is a great game that in hindsight did not deserve the backlash it got.
fun game i love the looks but its a bit laggy there and there but still a fun game

I recommend this game for people still wondering, "Is it worth the money?" I myself was still very skeptical if i should spend the money as I already had Cities: Skylines 1. I watched many videos of gameplay and comparison between each game, and I still was stuck between, what will be a better gameplay experience. I read many comments and none really answered the question. So in all, CS2 is better as it is more like you're actually building a city and you can make a whole city with the towns that's very realistic not just from graphic, but from gameplay. Now, they're definitely are some flaws that are better in CS1, like highways have signs, you can remove zones, etc. but CS1 is more of a game that's more unrealistic in how a city functions. And of course, it will have a lower quality in gameplay and graphic as it was made in 2015. It's also true, that a lot of mods can definitely enhance and "carry" CS2, but quit frankly for me the mods haven't buffed the experience as I haven't used them, but they can definitely knock CS1 out of the ball park when used and CS2 have mods built into the game.

(Refunded) I gave it a try after the slew of positive reviews from the most recent update that supposedly fixed a bunch of stuff.

Frankly, I wasn't affected by bugs, more by puzzling game design choices. I wouldn't recommend this game over Sklines 1 at this time.
It's honestly a really good game, and i don't really understand the mixed reviews. its a very big improvement compared to the first game in every way. A solid 9/10 for me.

I've been playing this game since launch, and it's always been a love-hate relationship. But over time, I've been enjoying it less and less.
For me, a game doesn't need to be perfect, which is also the reason why I gave Cities: Skylines 2 so many chances. But at this point, I'm just frustrated by all the crashes and performance issues the game still has.

I can see the improvements over time, but it's already been more than two and a half years since launch. At the moment, I just don't see myself enjoying the game anymore whenever I start it. You always have to wait absurd amounts of time for all your mods to be updated, to load into your city, and for the game itself to start at first, of course.
Usually, I'm a patient person, but my patience ends when I have to manually click through every error report and restart the game, only to repeat the whole process all over again because some mods are no longer compatible.
I'm looking forward to the improvements Iceflake Studios will provide, but for now, I'll continue my long-lasting break, hoping that one day I'll be able to start the game and actually enjoy it again.

If you're currently deciding whether the price is worth it, you should either wait a couple more years or give the game a try without expecting great performance unless your PC is at least mid-range. I played it at 12 FPS all low settings for about six months until I upgraded my PC, and now it's always a great heating alternative for my room.

Looking forward to giving Cities a thumbs up in the future.

I wanted to like this game, I really did. I put a lot of time in 1 and hoped for the best.

I bought it at launch and, unshockingly, refunded almost immediately. It was awful in ways that have been documented a lot on here from other comments. CO really screwed the pooch on this, and hopefully their CEO stays unemployed forever... toxicity at its finest.

Since they had a new developer, and some patched issues were notably fixed, I bought this again for $100. The UI was clunky, even on flagship hardware it was stuttering. The first game looked BETTER, which says a lot. The paradox mods being the only option to try and fix issues, but their mod platform being used to spread malware just... left me no hope.

After 30 minutes with the newest iteration I refunded. Spending $100 on a game that probably needs 5 years more work to be playable simply isn't worth it to me. Sooner, rather than later, Paradox will abandon this and I'd rather have my cash than another title in the library like KSP2
its a good game but when above 100k citizens, the game runs very poorly

Its an amazing game while modded. I use it as a city painter and with mods you can have endless creativity. Also, with the custom assets, it feels like new building blocks come into the game daily, which boosts creativity. There are still bugs and many mods that can easily be imcorperated into the game, but I still love to play it this way!

The recent Updates really improved the game. I played the first time through game pass. The game was a mere foundation of a city building game. The Updates they made really made the game work for me. If the new studio now fixes a few more things it will be great game.

This game is so hard to learn, the tutorial is not great at explaing how to maintain your city just the absolute basics. To start the game you have to watch 5 hours worth of tutorials and then once you understand how complex this game is you can't get a refund on it.

I got this game a month ago thinking surely the bugs are largely ironed out and they seemed to be. Now three weeks of completely broken Coal plants and mines as well as other broken delivery mechanisms and no hot fix. What the absolute unprofessional ****.
Somehow uses 100% of the gpu in the menu and runs at 3fps, at least for me. This game is absolute trash, you're better off playing Cities Skylines 1.

Almost 3 years after the release of CS2 the game is still in an embarrassingly unoptimized place. The FPS will randomly drop out of the sky down to 5fps for a period of 15-20 seconds before returning to normal. Which completely eviscerates any sense of immersion I have. Partner that with the fact that Paradox is just setting up another DLC shop instead of porting DLC from CS1 has caused me to lose all faith in this company. A company whos games I have played for many many years.

Do not buy this game.
Could have been better, after awhile it get redundant and becomes boring. It would be awesome if you could interact with the people or even have politics involved.

After thousands of hours in city builders and giving these folks nearly 3 years since launch to fix it....I will simply quote the studio's spokesperson.

"This game just might not be for you you."  Same girl.  Same.

But no really it's not for you.

8.5/10 Love it, Iceflake is better than Colossal Order. I would only recommend if you can deal with bugs and weird crap that doesn't make sense, but besides that and the usual paradox crap it's a great game!
Its a great game tbh, but I can't recommend it to people because of the fact that it keep crashing whenever you actually get a city to notible size.

The largest improvements over the original are: improved road construction tools, upgradable-expandable buildings, more realistic simulation, better graphics. 

The performance has been improved a lot but it will still require a powerful PC with a hefty CPU and GPU. Mine can handle about 330k citizens before it slows down dramatically, which is ok because in this game that equates to a very substantially sized city. 

Installling mods is easy but they are much more glitchy than in CS1, there are far fewer expansions and not as many things to do in this game but you can spend more time on watching and refining your cities growth, solving problems that feel more realistic rather than just pure volume of various buildings. 

I hoped the graphics would have been even better for all the money one needs to put into a PC to run the game well which is a big shame. The other big dissapointment is that there is still quite a lot of infrastructure that you can build but it is not clear how it directly affects your city. For example, if you build custom park areas with bike paths or leave more green space between residential zones, it doesn't seem to improve land value or make the buildings upgrade, improve happiness. It will make for better commutes although this is hard to see in actuality. It would be nice to see every layount and construction you make have a tangible impact. 

I still recommend the game but if your PC is older, you can be very happy with SC1 and all of its amazing expansions.

Extremely addictive and has really improved since its release. Although theres still a bit of content missing, as well as traffic ai and performance being a mess so if you don't have the recommended specs, do not waste your time.

Despite the flaws the game has, in my opinion its an upgrade from cities skylines 1, and it seems like its only getting better from here with the new updates (traffic ai and performance issues are actually being addressed). I would say the amount of content and mods available is what city skylines 1 does way better but other than that if you enjoy csl1 and have the recommended specs its worth getting.

(Need mods to enjoy)
Can't have a city over 500,000 without game chugging and max of 38fps. I love this game but If I can't even get to a million whats the point.

Started a new city to try the game out. Crashed in under 5 minutes. Didn't save. I'm getting a refund and playing the first game.

Edit 5/18/2026: Disable autosave that's what causes the freezing. This game is okay now if your pc can run it but still won't recommend it because you need a Paradox account to install their approved mods and there's no other official way to have mods.

Garbage. Hot Garbage. Somehow they managed to make a city simulator that both looks like crap and runs like crap. On a 5080/14700 the game runs terribly and is glitchy and flickers and the buildings look terrible. Skyscrapers have the same lighting on every window and at night you have to get within a few blocks for the lights to turn on. 

This late into this game for it to look and run so poorly is inexcusable. I waited for things to get better but it won't happen. I've been playing city simulators since the 80s. This isn't the city sim we wanted or needed. I can only hope somebody else makes something to fill the gap. 

Stick with CS1 which was only successful because of the modders.
Game is incredibly unstable practically unplayable with mods 10/10 would not recommend sucks because I LOVE CS1

This game has come a long way. I stopped playing for nearly a year and then Snowflake took over.  Snowflake has got this ship going in the right direction and the amount of mods created daily are really making it so you can create the city you see in your head. There's still things to fix but the future is bright.

simulation?      terrible. car crashes are just a RND not actual vehicle collisions. people just teleport. public 
                         transport sucks. whats a left turn lane? if they didnt added in PDX mods then I would 
                         have just stopped playing, because the game is so broken that I need 120+ mods to fix it.

graphics?         ehhh..  I dont really like it. it can look good, but honestly I think it just looks cheap. 
                        everything is flat, shadows lag the game like crazy, and sometimes it looks worse than 
                        base game minecraft.also i have never seen more than about 40 fps in a 5,000 pop+ city.

preformance?  do I even need to say anything about it, you already know it runs horribly.

models?           even in small cities you can see 10+ of the same skyscraper right next to each other. plus, 
                         they are all way to high poly and still look bad at the same time.

2/10 game functions (sometimes). only game I have that I would pirate DLC for
just play C:S 1

As a longtime Cities: Skylines player, I finally picked up Cities: Skylines II last week expecting a complete disaster based on what I'd read online. Instead, I found myself doing exactly what a city builder is supposed to make me do: losing track of time while obsessing over traffic, zoning, and infrastructure. Is the game perfect? Not even close. But somewhere between the criticism and the memes, people forgot that it's actually fun. I'd rather play an ambitious city builder with flaws than a polished one that plays it safe. The game still has work ahead of it, but if the biggest problem is that I sat down for an hour and accidentally played for six, I think it's doing something right.

I really like Cities: Skylines II. I grew up with SimCity 3000, so I tend to love city building games. The mechanics of this can seem overwhelming, but definitely make some things easier. (For example, electricity/water/sewer/etc. lines are built into the roads). Additionally, I love, so far at least, getting lost in all the possibilities building things out.
Great city builder although get it on sale whenever you can requires a beefy computer to run higher settings and in general big cities
What a turn around this game is having. Just played 4 hours and not a single crash. The game feels refreshed and much more polished. Excited to see what visions the new team brings.

The reason I like this game is that it has amazing graphics, is fun to play, and has a great theme. It's realistic and has a great concept. Overall, this game is a 10/10 for me. I love city-building games, so this was the perfect game for me. If you like realistic and sometimes challenging games, then you'll love this game. With all my heart, I fully recommend.

The game has improved a lot, but the only way you're getting a good game for its price is mods, the performance is bad, the gameplay/realism/looks of your town is lacking, mods fixes it by a lot so right now can't reccomend buying if completely new to this.

I’m playing Cities: Skylines and honestly can’t understand why it’s so boring. Even SimCity 2000 Deluxe had more things to do and felt more alive as a city-builder. It genuinely feels like the developers don’t understand what makes this genre interesting.

Why has nobody tried combining the depth of Transport Fever with the city planning of Cities: Skylines? Seriously, if Transport Fever allowed zoning like Cities does, Cities: Skylines would die overnight.

It’s such a painfully mediocre city-builder.

The only franchise that still consistently delivers is Anno — and that’s despite Ubisoft being behind it.

Without mods, there’s basically no reason to even play Cities: Skylines. It’s just not interesting.

I’m genuinely baffled by how awful the industrial zoning system is. Who thought this was acceptable design? Why are there no interactions with businesses or investors? Why can’t entrepreneurs approach the player with offers like: “I want to build a farm on this area, and in return I’ll provide taxes, jobs, and investment”? Where’s the actual economic simulation or meaningful decision-making?

You’d have to be unbelievably uninspired to create such a primitive city-builder in the 2020s.

I honestly don’t understand what I paid so much money for.

I could’ve just downloaded SimCity 2000 and had a far more engaging experience.

Even indie games like Manor Lords and Memoriapolis have more interesting and thoughtful city-building systems. At least those games have identity and ambition.

Cities: Skylines just feels like soulless garbage.

I’m honestly in disbelief.

This is just an incredibly primitive city-builder. We’re not living in the early ’90s or 2000s anymore, where people would happily accept gameplay this shallow and repetitive. It’s a complete disappointment.

What makes it even worse is that instead of acknowledging the core problems and making a genuinely better game, they keep trying to rehabilitate and patch over a fundamentally weak design.

Let me clarify something: I’m not even judging the optimization here. I can live with 20 FPS — I have a mid-range laptop, I wasn’t expecting miracles. My problem with Cities: Skylines is purely about gameplay.

If your only goal is to build a pretty city and take screenshots, then maybe you’ll enjoy it. Although even for that, you’ll probably end up installing mods for proper assets, road tools, and decent customization.

But personally, I enjoy city-builders for completely different reasons. I love managing profits, citizen satisfaction, economic balance, and long-term planning. I want the game to give me soft objectives and reward me for solving problems creatively.

Things like:
“Build a farm in this district and receive subsidies.”
“Allow heavy industry near the reservoir for higher tax income, but risk polluting the city’s water supply.”
“Support local businesses to increase employment.”
They could’ve added corporations (not just transport companies) like in OpenTTD, competing with each other over cities, proposing initiatives, lobbying different communities, and so on. Like, one group wants ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bike lanes, while these oil-loving dudes want more parking spaces for their absurdly huge semis and trucks.

So basically, where’s the conflict between environmentalists and car addicts, corporations and farmers? Where are the protests from unhappy citizens? This isn’t city-building — it’s just urban planning for a pretty screenshot.


That’s actual gameplay.

Those kinds of systems already existed in older strategy games like Zeus + Poseidon, Caesar, Pharaoh, and even that amazing ancient China city-builder I can’t remember the name of right now. Even back then, these games tried to simulate meaningful governance and city management.

Meanwhile, Cities: Skylines boils down to this:

Paint zones.
Optimize traffic.
Raise taxes.
Congratulations: +$13,371,488,322 per second.

That’s it.

It feels incredibly shallow.

There’s barely any meaningful control over city development. Where are district-specific incentives? Where are subsidies? Where are policies designed for individual neighborhoods? Why are taxes mostly tied to the entire city instead of specialized districts and local economies?

Everything feels oversimplified to the point of absurdity.

And the funniest part is that the only decent assets are mostly locked behind DLCs — but honestly, even those aren’t worth it. You’re better off using mods because most of the official content feels just as mediocre.

So honestly:
buy the game only if it’s massively discounted.
Or pirate it first just to understand how empty it actually is.

I genuinely don’t understand what they spent all those years developing.
Everywhere you look, it feels unfinished and primitive:
poor optimization,
shallow gameplay,
barely any real simulation.

Sorry for the wall of text, but this game genuinely pissed me off. Especially because I bought this ♥♥♥♥ with all the DLCs.
just spent 5 hours building a city, just for the game to crash and it not auto save. so its gone for ever. 10 out 10! Its a must have game if you want to shoot you're self. :)

Used to play Cities all the time, everyday all day. Now every time i try and play I get the blue screen of death. I've uninstalled and reinstalled i cant tell you how many times. I've disabled MOD's, I've done everything i can think of and nothing.  Extremally disappointing because this is a great game... if it works.

dont buy this garbage, seriously just play the first one. this one is straight up robbery. runs like ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ even on high end PCs. i quit for a year and came back to it now and it's like nothing changed except a ton of expensive DLC has been added, things that should've already been in the game since launch.

let this game die and be forgotten
the game plays itself you cannot play the game. I disabled the crosswalk and put a pedestrian overpass and still people jump to the road.

I am giving this a Not Recommend for now in the interest  that this game continues to improve. At launch, I refunded the game because of performance issues, but in its current state I believe that for casual players like me who just enjoy balancing supply chains and building a giant urban sprawl, this game is a good purchase on a sale. That being said, there are a couple annoying items that detract from the fun, most notably with managing zoning on specific tiles and not having a way to change out industry supply chains aside from demolishing over and over until you get the company (and product) you want. Overall if you're looking to upgrade from CS1 and aren't invested in modding, this is worth your time, but it still needs more improvements to be truly special. I am having fun. However, for every improvement and depth Cities Skyline 2 adds on top of Cities Skylines 1, it also seemingly removes control and buries information in abstract ways, removing agency from the player. This doesn't need to continue to be the case and I belive the current developers will make this game better.

Since the update on 5 May, the game has deteriorated significantly. I used to play on high graphics settings with a good frame rate, but after the update, all the optimisation suddenly disappeared. The frame rate is still comfortable, but much lower now. But that’s not the main issue. You’ve completely ruined the export and energy economies. All industries: oil, fish, grain, and so on, including special buildings, cannot export goods. A lorry appears and immediately returns to the factory. Resources aren’t being shipped. There’s no production, and factories in the red. Coal-fired power stations cannot import coal and are also dying out. I used to love your company. I have almost all ur games. I didn’t expect you to be capable of producing such poor-quality, strange things. Shame on you, this is simply unacceptable for your company and your reputation.
My game won't load mods anymore. The game is incredibly fun and I enjoy playing it. but it is entirely a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stupid unstable and unplayable

While i do like the base game additions such as trams which weren't in the original. The same clunky placement system for roads remains, and it seems worse, intersections always look ugly with stretched models. Also the lag is significantly worse, with it running at 50fps on an empty map.

WHY DO THE PEDESTRIANS WALK ON TRAM AND HIGHWAY TRACKS???? One thing about the trams is there is no option for it to be a light rail or tram option, that is, have its own right of way and dedicated stations(the existing stations take up too much space, please look at Sydney Light Rail L1 for reference for a good light rail station designs with underground options.) rather than "stops". The tram must mix with traffic in this game, and the only way to avoid that is to place tracks standalone, there is no choice of making it similar to Sydney Light Rail and how it has its own dedicated lanes that cars cannot drive over. That is genuinely stupid, i dont want my trams mixing with car traffic.

PEDESTRIANS... why do they jaywalk constantly. I could add a pedestrian bridge and remove the cross walks yet theyll just run across the intersection and bottle up traffic(they do this on highways too, theyll cross a highway exit and cause slowdowns, ANNOYING)

I hate this game, and I hate the first one too. The developers, I don't know if they're overworked(sorry if so) but this has to be the most unoptimised, most convoluted that i've ever played. The controls are clunky, routing for public transport is confusing and it trams will randomly derail, delete itself and deposit all passengers onto the stop. This is the one game I hope gets a competitor, while it is fun to play, there are so many things that can be improved. 

Don't buy, I think I've seen roblox games more optimised than this....

I have never written a review on a game before but this is annoying. I've tried really hard to like this game. Added mods, ect. But the one thing that is making me hate this game is the traffic. With the first game, I was able to fix the intersections to make traffic flow nicely. But with this, there are so many things that are making traffic worse. 

Roundabouts: have pedestrian crossings but do not allow you to take them out or add lights. This makes it so traffic is always at a standstill because there are so many pedestrians. They refuse to take the bridge so not even making one is helpful. 

Uturns: cars are now making unnecessary uturns right in the middle of the street which holds up traffic. No ability to add fines/make illegal. 

They took out all of the city policies so that isn't an option to have city wide policies. Which I personally liked from the old game. 

Had to add a mod in order to adjust the lights, which is fine, however it would be nice to be able to adjust the lights to be timed better to allow the busiest road to go for longer than the lanes sitting empty. 

If they just focused on fixing the traffic, this would be an amazing game, better than the old one. But it still has a very long way to go.

Positive Things
- I do like how they added tools which are easy to use, though, like the roundabout tool, for example.
- The graphics look really nice! They took some time on the details of the buildings and it really shows.

Negative Things
- I don't like how you can't download assets/mods from the Steam Workshop anymore, as compared to the first one. Just downloading mods from a secondary site (paradoxplaza.com) is just one step in adding them. Then there are additional settings inside the game that you have to install to get them to work. It seems way less user-friendly and an overall downgrade.
- After a few hours, I run out of things to build and it feels like I'm just building the same things over and over again. The game has been out for 3 years now and only has one DLC expansion pack and even with that, it feels bare. The other creator packs just makes things look different and doesn't add anything else outside of the normal game to experience. I feel like I lost so much content going from CS1 to CS2.
- The game, despite having a great computer, lags and feels very choppy. I've attached my PC specs.

I will continue to check back on the game because I think there's still hope for it. I know they've gone through a rough time with getting passed from one company to the next. There's a lot probably happening in the background that we don't know about and I know that there are devs there that truly want to make this game better.

The traffic in this game is worse than CS 1 I have been trying for the last 13 hours trying to find the root cause of it and installed multiple addons to fix the issues yet this game is not ready for release I highly suggest you to stick to CS 1 it is more accessible and has better traffic than CS2 also running this game makes your pc go jet mode you can hear the gpu screaming from how high graphics this game is I don't suggest anyone buying this game until these issues are fixed most importantly the traffic engine
This game is demanding and requires the best rig you can put together. I love this game and am happy that I have a system that can handle the strain.

The best city building simulation game ever created. Can get lost for hours playing this. 10/10 would recommend, however, some of the DLC content such as the "Bridges and Ports" should be added as a part of the base game. More customisable traffic/road routes would be good too. The ability to adjust and change the direction of certain lanes should be featured.
This game is really good and it is a great standalone game while it has many features I think are better than the original the original game is far more solid

Overall I don't think it deserves the hate that it gets. works fine for me (I7 12790k, 3070ti). Skylines 1 has more content but of course it does since it's over 10 yrs old. The radio in this game is the most annoying thing about it. repetetive and radio commentary is enough to force you to wuit. turn the music off and listen to your own tunes
unfortunately its not playable at all. im really sorry to say that i cannot play this game right now. i hope they fix the optimization
After 3 years from being released you would think this game is much better developed but it's definitely got a long way to go
I love this game, its amazing but it just has too many issues and bugs. this game is in complete, I wish yall would finish it because it has so much potential

A bit difficult to grasp at first, but once you get used to the mechanics very fun!

There is a few graphical glitches (e.g. entities having a "trail" behind them), but they don't impact the game too much.

It's still pretty buggy, but if you can ignore the bugs, I recommend.

Performance feels comparable to the first version of the game. Simulation runs smoothly and then hits some hiccups at around 100k population-- nothing major, mostly just slows simulation speed down regardless of your selected setting.

Tourism is busted.
its not stalking if you built the place. they get roads. i get to watch them cause a traffic jam 19 miles long. win win

For the most part, the core game mechanics are much better in comparison to the first game. But as of right now there just isn't enough content, both player made and developer made. Stick with Cities Skylines 1 for a while or until the game is on sale.
Good game, great sequel to the original. Only thing is especially with mods, it can be quite taxing on your hardware. Definitely check the system requirement before buying if you haven't already.

Finally better than the original. Lots of good mod support. Lots of good expansion and dlc. Say what you will about Paradox's DLC business model but there is plenty of both official and mod content for this game now. I waited to buy this until I was sure and I'm happy with the purchase.
It's certainly come around. Not perfect but the new developers have definitely steered it back on course

Love this game, was playing through the game pass but recently bought this. Love the micro management aspect of the game from managing road traffic and foot traffic while keeping the productions side of the city in check as well.

it's a good game but one big building will cause traffic jams just to get to it and the ai just stay there when you lane is open but you can can upgrade change where ships go and a some tips if you want to have a good city 1.your main roads should be big 2.to get the mail rating to go up get a cargo port 3.for a normal port get a tram. 4.buses need a bus station for it to stop at or any smaller bus stations
Since turning over to IceFlake Studios this game has made great strides and looks to get better. I played CS1 for 3000 hrs, now have over 1000 in this entry and I can't go back to the original.
looks like they've patched most of the bugs everyone was talking about. It's a great game!
It's like the original Cities: Skylines but with useful city building. For example, electricity is under the road along with water and sewer pipes and overall, just much more cusomization.

The initial release of this game was super rocky, but now that Iceflake Studios is working on it, I finally feel like it is heading in the right direction. They have been fixing bugs and performance issues all over the place and now it is pretty fun to play the game. There are also tons of plans for new expansions, but even the base game is fun without any of the DLC. I'd recommend it now to give it a shot if you want something more modern than Cities Skylines 1.
the last time i played a game like this was rollercoaster tycoon and i loved it and this beats it out of the park!

The new developers are actually turning the game around. While I would not have recommended the game while it was still developed by Colossal Order, the new devs are quick to implement fixes whenever issues arise and seem to care much more for the quality of the game. The game still has quite a way to go, but I'm optimistic for the future and would recommend the game to anyone who was still uncertain.
Its not without its flaws, but I have to commend just how engaging the gameplay is.
Expect alot of bugs but you can use reddit to help fix those bugs but if you have high patience i think this is a very good game. PS: You need a better pc for this
Love building unique cities and messing about. Better road tools than Cities Skylines 1. Performance is lacking. Still prefer it to the first title overall but its certainly not for everyone.
So far it's the only game I didn't drop yet. It's huge, but everything is fun and worth to try.
It is fun if you are bored. Lots to do in the game but there isn't much to brag to your friends about.

My game has been crashing a lot recently, for no reason. I'm just playing, building a brand new city with 0 population, however it just crashes for no reason. As you can see my specs are perfectly compatible to play with no issues. Please fix the damn game.

Cities: Skylines 2 looks fresh and modern. However, it puts too much strain on your CPU and GPU for what you get. The performance dips make building a big city less enjoyable. Right now, stick with Cities Skylines 1
Very fun and engaging. I easliy lose track of time and feel calm while playing.

So i didnt plan for this review to be so long, but It ended up being quite long... oops.
at first i might have given this game an OK review... but my game suddenly crashes (just leaves the game) so many times, at least once every 3 or so hours of playing. theres nothing wrong with my PC, its high powered, and I dont have any weird, old or bad mods, only the usual like Anarchy, the different housing themes, etc. Twice, since I've played it recently, I've started a new city, played for a couple of hours, then its crashed and I have lost ALL of my progress - hours of work. Maybe if it had happened once or twice over a longer period it would have been fine, but twice in less than three days? loosing ALL of my progress?
is there no constant (every 10-30 mins) autosave in the game?

Additionally to continue on about the mods, particularly the different zoning housing themes, they dont fill all the area well, unlike the base ones? i understand the houses dont have all the variations (1 by 1 to 6 by 6). If you have any sugestions for that please tell me.

Lastly, the traffic system and housing numbers are so out of wack. It requires you to build such a large area of low density housing for only a small amount of facilites/other zoning. the Ai traffic is frankly terrible choosing the worst paths and not using freeways, and there are so many for just a small number of houses. I know a lot of this may technically be realistic because we need a lot of low dencity housing and have a large reliance of cars irl, but is it better to be realistic or better for the player, while still being slightly realistic. also, just wondering if you can fix the traffic with transport, like if you had more public transport, will the amount of traffic decrease? 
(p.s. why is there so much foot traffic???)
(p.s.s why are all the good things in the buyable mods (e.g. fishing, harbors, cool bridges, tourist stuff)???, can we get some more stuff in the base game please??? Can we normalise not having 1000 DLC's? like the new game paralives, i understand people have to get their bag, and they're not indi developers, but damn can we alease get some other types of zoned buildings other than europe and america(+there is only one type of low density commercial zones?!?!?!), more tourist stuff and more than one harbor/airport type/size pleaaaase???)

Other than that, the game has a lot of poteintal, not unlike the first one - they're very similar, I'd even say this one is a downgrade. the graphics are great, the road system are good, and the maps are great. my favorite things are the varity of the low density housing (+others), the ability to get mods in the game that download easily and not having to pay for terraforming (+ the terraforming system is very good). I hope with this new devs the game will improve, and i am hopeful.

Overall, I cant wait to see where this game goes, i wouldn't recomend people to get the game in its current state (unless its on a big sale - which is what I did), but in the future it should be a definite.
